REPLY IN SUPPORT OF DEFTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY DEFENDANT SIMMS, ANN UNITED HEALTHCARE SERVICES, INC. UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- DEFENDANTS ANN SIMMS, UNITED HEALTHCARE SERVICES, INCORPORATED AND UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED AND THE ACTION IS STAYED, ALTHOUGH PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE, THE OFFENDING CLAUSES CAN BE SEVERED AND DO NOT PERMIT THE AGREEMENT. THE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF CLAUSE IS SEVERED, BUT DOES NOT MATERIALLY CHANGE THE PARTIES RIGHTS GIVEN THAT C C P SECTION 128.18 STILL GIVES THEM A SIMILAR ABILITY TO GO TO COURT. THE AMENDMENT OR TERMINATION CLAUSE DOES NOT SUGGEST IMPOSITION OF A LESSER FORUM AND IS SEVERED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: ARLENE HOWELL, CSR # 2542 301/PJB
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-21-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAYING THE CIVIL ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-11-2011 CONTINUED TO SEP-16-2011 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR STATUS OF ARBITRATION STAY. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF SEP-16-2011 CONTINUED TO JAN-20-2012 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR STATUS OF ARBITRATION STAY. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JAN-20-2012 IS OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Andrea de Koning, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw, 415-397-2823 for defendants and moving parties
Felipe Parker, Esq. of Kumin Sommers, 415-434-4500 for plaintiff and opposing party Robert Norgaard

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANTS ANN SIMMS, UNITED HEALTHCARE SERVICES, INCORPORATED AND UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, INCORPORATED'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration, ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED AND THE ACTION IS STAYED, ALTHOUGH PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE, THE OFFENDING CLAUSES CAN BE SEVERED AND DO NOT PERMIT THE AGREEMENT. THE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF CLAUSE IS SEVERED, BUT DOES NOT MATERIALLY CHANGE THE PARTIES RIGHTS GIVEN THAT C C P SECTION 128.18 STILL GIVES THEM A SIMILAR ABILITY TO GO TO COURT. THE AMENDMENT OR TERMINATION CLAUSE DOES NOT SUGGEST IMPOSITION OF A LESSER FORUM AND IS SEVERED. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR # 2542 301/PJB
